coreIssue: This 1:1 is likely about whether a Director-level promotion setback has changed James's engagement and confidence in his path forward.
openingQuestion: What feedback from the promotion decision has stayed with you most, and how is it shaping what you want to lean into or step back from now?
listenFor: whether he names a specific part of the promotion feedback without prompting or stays vague
if they mention a recent ritual, meeting, or forum they have pulled back from and why
if they describe the mentoring program with concrete examples tied to product leadership scope or as relief from core Head of Product work
avoid: do not frame his lower participation as a motivation problem before you hear his read on it
do not treat the mentoring program as proof of growth or avoidance without testing how he sees it
goodOutcome: You and James have agreed one concrete Director-level path forward, including the kind of Head of Product scope or leadership behaviour that would show readiness for the next promotion case.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one clear example of his changed participation in a team ritual and one exact piece of promotion feedback so you can test specifics instead of speaking in generalities.
